sources.quote: Occupational therapy. To help improve your ability to perform daily living skills such as eating, drinking, bathing, and dressing.

factsExtracted.value: Stroke rehabilitation is a program for people who have had a stroke; occupational therapy helps improve daily living skills such as eating, drinking, bathing, and dressing.
Therapists work with patients on daily living skills including eating, drinking, bathing, and dressing.
Stroke rehabilitation can help you relearn skills you lost because of the damage.
